title: Tarenna affinis
taxonomy: Tarenna affinis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family includes a wide range of species, many of which are economically and medicinally important. Within the family Rubiaceae, Tarenna affinis is further classified as a member of the genus Tarenna, which comprises about 200 species of plants. The genus Tarenna is part of the tribe Palicoureeae, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia and Africa. The classification of Tarenna affinis reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants within its family and genus.

intro: Tarenna affinis, also known as Indian Tarenna or East Himalayan Tarenna, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which provide relief from pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Tarenna affinis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Tarenna affinis is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as topical creams and ointments, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
